certificate.en: an official document that proves a fact, qualification, or completion of a course

ex.en: She received a certificate after completing the training course.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교육 과정을 마친 후 수료증을 받았다.

certificate.en: an official document that proves ownership of stocks, bonds, or other financial assets

ex.en: He kept the old stock certificate in a safe.
ex.ko: 그는 오래된 주식 증권을 금고에 보관했다.

certification.en: the act or document confirming that someone or something meets an official standard

ex.en: She got certification in first aid last year.
ex.ko: 그녀는 작년에 응급처치 자격증명을 받았다.

certify.en: officially state that something is true or meets a required standard

ex.en: The lab certified that the water was safe to drink.
ex.ko: 그 연구소는 물이 마시기에 안전하다고 증명했다.

certify.en: officially recognize someone as qualified to do a particular job

ex.en: He was certified as a professional diver last month.
ex.ko: 그는 지난달 전문 다이버 자격을 인가받았다.

chancellor.en: the head of government in certain countries, such as Germany

ex.en: The German chancellor gave a speech about the economy.
ex.ko: 독일 총리는 경제에 관한 연설을 했다.

chancellor.en: the honorary or ceremonial head of a university

ex.en: The chancellor handed out diplomas at graduation.
ex.ko: 총장은 졸업식에서 학위증을 나눠주었다.

chaotic.en: in a state of complete disorder and confusion

ex.en: Traffic was chaotic after the accident.
ex.ko: 사고 이후 교통이 혼란스러웠다.

ex.en: She has a charming smile.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매력적인 미소를 가지고 있다.

cheat.en: act dishonestly to gain an advantage, especially in a test or game

ex.en: He was caught cheating on the exam.
ex.ko: 그는 시험에서 부정행위를 하다가 걸렸다.

cheat.en: trick someone in order to get money or something valuable from them

ex.en: The salesman cheated her out of her savings.
ex.ko: 그 판매원은 그녀를 속여 저축한 돈을 빼앗았다.

cheat.en: be sexually unfaithful to your partner

ex.en: She found out he had been cheating on her.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그가 바람을 피워왔다는 것을 알게 되었다.

cheat.en: a person who behaves dishonestly to gain an advantage

ex.en: Everyone at the club knew he was a cheat.
ex.ko: 클럽의 모두가 그가 사기꾼이라는 것을 알고 있었다.

checkpoint.en: a place where people or vehicles are stopped and inspected

ex.en: Soldiers stood guard at the checkpoint.
ex.ko: 군인들이 검문소에서 보초를 섰다.

cheeky.en: rude or disrespectful in a bold, often amusing way

ex.en: The cheeky kid stuck his tongue out at the teacher.
ex.ko: 그 건방진 아이는 선생님에게 혀를 내밀었다.

cheerfully.en: in a happy and positive way

ex.en: She greeted everyone cheerfully at the door.
ex.ko: 그녀는 문 앞에서 모두를 쾌활하게 맞이했다.

cheerfulness.en: the quality of being happy and positive

ex.en: Her cheerfulness lifted everyone's mood.
ex.ko: 그녀의 쾌활함이 모두의 기분을 좋게 만들었다.

chemotherapy.en: the treatment of cancer using powerful chemical drugs

ex.en: She is undergoing chemotherapy for breast cancer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 유방암 화학요법을 받고 있다.

cherished.en: held very dear or valued deeply

ex.en: This ring is a cherished family heirloom.
ex.ko: 이 반지는 소중히 여겨지는 가보이다.

cherry.en: a small, round, red fruit with a hard stone inside

ex.en: She bought a bag of fresh cherries.
ex.ko: 그녀는 신선한 체리 한 봉지를 샀다.

cherry.en: a tree that produces cherries or is grown for its blossoms

ex.en: Cherry trees bloom beautifully in spring.
ex.ko: 벚나무는 봄에 아름답게 꽃을 피운다.

chew.en: crush food with your teeth before swallowing

ex.en: You should chew your food slowly.
ex.ko: 음식을 천천히 씹어야 한다.

childish.en: behaving in a silly or immature way, not suitable for an adult

ex.en: Stop being so childish about losing the game.
ex.ko: 게임에서 진 것 가지고 그렇게 유치하게 굴지 마.

chimney.en: a pipe that carries smoke away from a fire through the roof

ex.en: Smoke rose from the chimney.
ex.ko: 굴뚝에서 연기가 피어올랐다.

chip.en: a small piece broken off something

ex.en: There's a chip in this plate.
ex.ko: 이 접시에 조각이 떨어져 나갔다.

chip.en: a small electronic device that performs computing functions

ex.en: This phone has a powerful new chip inside.
ex.ko: 이 휴대폰에는 강력한 새 칩이 들어 있다.

chip.en: a small disk used to represent money in gambling

ex.en: He bought a stack of chips at the casino.
ex.ko: 그는 카지노에서 칩 한 무더기를 샀다.

chip.en: break a small piece off the edge of something

ex.en: Be careful not to chip the wine glasses.
ex.ko: 와인잔이 깨지지 않게 조심해.

chop.en: cut something into smaller pieces with a knife or axe

ex.en: She chopped the onions for the soup.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수프에 넣을 양파를 썰었다.

chop.en: a small piece of meat, often including part of a rib

ex.en: We had pork chops for dinner.
ex.ko: 우리는 저녁으로 돼지고기 찹을 먹었다.

chunk.en: a large, solid piece or amount of something

ex.en: He cut off a chunk of bread.
ex.ko: 그는 빵을 큰 조각으로 잘라냈다.

circuit.en: a complete path that an electric current can flow through

ex.en: A short circuit caused the fire.
ex.ko: 합선(회로 이상)이 화재를 일으켰다.

circuit.en: a route that goes around a series of places and returns to the start

ex.en: The band went on a concert circuit across Europe.
ex.ko: 그 밴드는 유럽 전역을 순회하는 공연을 다녔다.

circuitry.en: the complete system of electronic circuits inside a device

ex.en: The engineer repaired the damaged circuitry.
ex.ko: 기술자는 손상된 회로를 수리했다.

circumstance.en: a fact or condition connected with an event or situation

ex.en: Under the circumstances, we had no choice but to cancel.
ex.ko: 그런 상황에서 우리는 취소할 수밖에 없었다.

citywide.en: happening or existing across an entire city

ex.en: A citywide power outage affected millions of people.
ex.ko: 시 전역의 정전이 수백만 명에게 영향을 미쳤다.

clam.en: a shellfish with a hard shell that lives in sand or mud

ex.en: We dug for clams on the beach.
ex.ko: 우리는 해변에서 조개를 캤다.

clap.en: hit your hands together to show approval or make a sound

ex.en: The audience clapped loudly after the show.
ex.ko: 공연이 끝난 후 관객들이 크게 박수를 쳤다.

clarify.en: make something clearer or easier to understand

ex.en: Could you clarify what you mean by that?
ex.ko: 그게 무슨 뜻인지 명확히 해줄 수 있어?

classification.en: the act of arranging things into groups based on shared features

ex.en: The classification of animals is based on their characteristics.
ex.ko: 동물의 분류는 그들의 특징을 바탕으로 한다.

classification.en: an official restriction on documents that limits access to authorized people

ex.en: The report's classification was changed from secret to public.
ex.ko: 그 보고서의 기밀 등급이 비밀에서 공개로 바뀌었다.

clause.en: (grammar) a group of words containing a subject and a verb

ex.en: This sentence has two clauses joined by 'and'.
ex.ko: 이 문장은 'and'로 연결된 두 개의 절을 가지고 있다.

clause.en: a separate section of a legal document such as a contract

ex.en: Read the contract carefully — there's a cancellation clause.
ex.ko: 계약서를 꼼꼼히 읽어봐 — 해지 조항이 있어.

clearness.en: the quality of being easy to understand or see through

ex.en: He explained the plan with great clearness.
ex.ko: 그는 아주 명료하게 계획을 설명했다.

clerical.en: relating to routine office work such as filing and typing

ex.en: She made a clerical error in the report.
ex.ko: 그녀는 보고서에서 사무적인(문서상의) 실수를 했다.

clerical.en: relating to priests or the clergy

ex.en: He wore clerical robes during the ceremony.
ex.ko: 그는 의식 동안 성직자 예복을 입었다.

client.en: a person who pays for goods or services, especially professional advice

ex.en: The lawyer met with her client this morning.
ex.ko: 변호사는 오늘 아침 의뢰인을 만났다.

client.en: (computing) a computer or program that requests services from a server

ex.en: Each client connects to the central server.
ex.ko: 각 클라이언트는 중앙 서버에 접속한다.

climate change.en: a long-term change in the world's weather patterns, especially due to human activity

ex.en: Climate change is affecting sea levels worldwide.
ex.ko: 기후 변화는 전 세계 해수면에 영향을 미치고 있다.

clog.en: become or make something blocked so that nothing can pass through

ex.en: Hair often clogs the shower drain.
ex.ko: 머리카락이 자주 샤워 배수구를 막는다.

closure.en: the permanent shutting down of a business, place, or operation

ex.en: The factory closure left hundreds jobless.
ex.ko: 그 공장 폐쇄로 수백 명이 실직했다.

closure.en: a feeling that a difficult experience has been resolved and can be left behind

ex.en: Talking about her loss gave her a sense of closure.
ex.ko: 상실에 대해 이야기하는 것이 그녀에게 마음의 매듭을 지어주었다.

clothing.en: things that people wear to cover their bodies

ex.en: She packed warm clothing for the trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행을 위해 따뜻한 옷을 챙겼다.

clumsy.en: moving or doing things in an awkward way, often dropping or breaking things

ex.en: He's a bit clumsy and often trips over his own feet.
ex.ko: 그는 좀 서투른 편이라 자주 자기 발에 걸려 넘어진다.

coaching.en: the job or activity of teaching and training someone to improve a skill

ex.en: She offers coaching for new managers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 신임 관리자를 위한 코칭을 제공한다.

coalition.en: a temporary alliance formed by different groups or parties working toward a common goal

ex.en: The two parties formed a coalition government.
ex.ko: 두 정당은 연립 정부를 구성했다.

coarse.en: rough in texture, not smooth or fine

ex.en: The sweater was made of coarse wool.
ex.ko: 그 스웨터는 거친 양모로 만들어졌다.

coarse.en: rude or vulgar in speech or manner

ex.en: He made a coarse joke that offended everyone.
ex.ko: 그는 모두를 불쾌하게 만드는 저속한 농담을 했다.

coastline.en: the shape of the land along the edge of the sea

ex.en: The coastline stretches for miles.
ex.ko: 그 해안선은 수 마일에 걸쳐 뻗어 있다.

cocktail.en: a mixed alcoholic drink made from two or more ingredients

ex.en: She ordered a fruity cocktail at the bar.
ex.ko: 그녀는 바에서 과일향 칵테일을 주문했다.

cocktail.cue: 식사 전에 나오는 새우 등 전채
cocktail.en: a small dish of seafood or fruit served as a starter before a meal

ex.en: We started with a shrimp cocktail.
ex.ko: 우리는 새우 칵테일로 식사를 시작했다.

cocoa.en: a hot drink made from cocoa powder, milk, and sugar

ex.en: She made a cup of cocoa on a cold night.
ex.ko: 그녀는 추운 밤에 코코아 한 잔을 만들었다.

coconut.en: a large brown fruit with a hard shell and white flesh, from a palm tree

ex.en: He drank fresh coconut water on the beach.
ex.ko: 그는 해변에서 신선한 코코넛 물을 마셨다.

coherence.en: the quality of ideas or a piece of writing fitting together logically

ex.en: The essay lacked coherence between paragraphs.
ex.ko: 그 에세이는 문단 간 일관성이 부족했다.

coherent.en: logical and well organized, easy to follow

ex.en: He gave a coherent explanation of the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 사고에 대해 논리 정연한 설명을 했다.

coincide.en: happen at the same time as something else

ex.en: Her birthday coincides with the national holiday.
ex.ko: 그녀의 생일은 국경일과 겹친다.

coincide.en: be exactly the same as or agree with something

ex.en: Our opinions on this issue coincide perfectly.
ex.ko: 이 문제에 대한 우리의 의견은 완전히 일치한다.

coincidence.en: a situation in which two things happen at the same time by chance

ex.en: What a coincidence to meet you here!
ex.ko: 여기서 널 만나다니 정말 우연의 일치네!

colleague.en: a person you work with, especially in a professional job

ex.en: I had lunch with a colleague from work.
ex.ko: 나는 직장 동료와 점심을 먹었다.

collector.en: a person who collects things such as stamps or coins as a hobby

ex.en: He is a passionate art collector.
ex.ko: 그는 열정적인 미술품 수집가이다.

collector.en: a person whose job is to collect money such as taxes or fares

ex.en: The rent collector came by every month.
ex.ko: 집세 수금원이 매달 찾아왔다.

collocation.en: a combination of words that are commonly used together, such as 'make a decision'

ex.en: 'Heavy rain' is a common collocation in English.
ex.ko: 'heavy rain'은 영어에서 흔히 쓰이는 연어이다.

colon.en: a punctuation mark (:) used before a list, example, or explanation

ex.en: Use a colon before listing examples.
ex.ko: 예시를 나열하기 전에 콜론을 사용하세요.

colon.en: the main part of the large intestine

ex.en: The doctor recommended a colon screening.
ex.ko: 의사는 대장 검진을 권했다.

colonial.en: relating to a colony or the period when a country ruled over colonies

ex.en: The museum displays colonial-era furniture.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 식민 시대 가구를 전시한다.

coma.en: a deep, prolonged state of unconsciousness, often caused by injury or illness

ex.en: He was in a coma for two weeks after the accident.
ex.ko: 그는 사고 후 2주 동안 혼수상태였다.

comfortably.en: in a way that feels relaxed, physically at ease, or without financial worry

ex.en: They live comfortably on a modest income.
ex.ko: 그들은 적당한 수입으로도 편안하게 산다.

commander.en: a person in charge of a military unit or operation

ex.en: The commander gave orders to the troops.
ex.ko: 지휘관은 부대에 명령을 내렸다.

commemorate.en: remember and honor an important event or person, often with a ceremony

ex.en: The statue commemorates the soldiers who died in the war.
ex.ko: 그 동상은 전쟁에서 전사한 군인들을 기념한다.

commerce.en: the activity of buying and selling goods and services, especially on a large scale

ex.en: The internet has transformed global commerce.
ex.ko: 인터넷은 전 세계 상업을 변화시켰다.

commission.en: formally ask someone to produce a piece of work for you

ex.en: The city commissioned an artist to paint a mural.
ex.ko: 시는 한 예술가에게 벽화 제작을 의뢰했다.

commission.en: a percentage of money paid to someone for selling something or arranging a deal

ex.en: She earns a 10% commission on every sale.
ex.ko: 그녀는 판매 건마다 10%의 수수료를 받는다.

commission.en: an official group of people given the task of investigating or overseeing something

ex.en: The commission investigated the cause of the crash.
ex.ko: 위원회는 그 추락 사고의 원인을 조사했다.

commonwealth.en: a group of independent countries or states associated for mutual benefit

ex.en: Australia is a member of the Commonwealth.
ex.ko: 호주는 영연방의 일원이다.

communicative.en: relating to the exchange of information between people

ex.en: The course focuses on communicative skills in English.
ex.ko: 그 과정은 영어 의사소통 능력에 초점을 맞춘다.

communicative.en: willing and able to talk to people easily and share information

ex.en: He's usually quiet but very communicative with close friends.
ex.ko: 그는 평소엔 조용하지만 친한 친구들과는 대화를 아주 잘 나눈다.

community.en: the people who live in a particular area

ex.en: The whole community came together to clean the park.
ex.ko: 지역사회 전체가 공원을 청소하기 위해 힘을 모았다.

community.en: a group of people who share a characteristic, interest, or belief

ex.en: She is active in the online gaming community.
ex.ko: 그녀는 온라인 게임 커뮤니티에서 활발히 활동한다.

commute.en: travel regularly between home and work

ex.en: He commutes to the city every day by train.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 기차로 도시까지 통근한다.

commute.en: the regular journey you make between home and work

ex.en: Her commute takes about an hour.
ex.ko: 그녀의 통근 시간은 약 한 시간이 걸린다.

compassion.en: a strong feeling of sympathy for someone who is suffering

ex.en: She treated the patients with great compassion.
ex.ko: 그녀는 환자들을 큰 연민으로 대했다.

compassionate.en: showing sympathy and concern for people who are suffering

ex.en: The nurse was compassionate toward every patient.
ex.ko: 그 간호사는 모든 환자에게 동정심을 가지고 대했다.

compatible.en: able to be used together with another device or system without problems

ex.en: This charger is compatible with most phones.
ex.ko: 이 충전기는 대부분의 휴대폰과 호환된다.

compatible.ko: 양립할 수 있는, 서로 잘 맞는
compatible.en: able to exist or work together well with someone or something else

ex.en: Their personalities are surprisingly compatible.
ex.ko: 그들의 성격은 놀라울 정도로 잘 맞는다.

compensate.en: pay someone money for a loss, injury, or damage

ex.en: The airline compensated passengers for the delay.
ex.ko: 항공사는 승객들에게 지연에 대해 보상했다.

compensate.en: balance out a disadvantage or lack with something positive

ex.en: Her enthusiasm compensates for her lack of experience.
ex.ko: 그녀의 열정이 경험 부족을 메운다.

compensation.en: money paid to someone because of a loss, injury, or damage

ex.en: He received compensation for his workplace injury.
ex.ko: 그는 업무상 부상에 대한 보상금을 받았다.

competence.en: the ability to do something well or successfully

ex.en: His competence in handling crises impressed the board.
ex.ko: 위기 대처 능력이 이사회에 깊은 인상을 남겼다.

competent.en: having the skill and knowledge to do something well

ex.en: She is a competent and reliable manager.
ex.ko: 그녀는 유능하고 믿음직한 관리자이다.

compile.en: collect information from different sources and put it together in one document or list

ex.en: She compiled a list of useful websites.
ex.ko: 그녀는 유용한 웹사이트 목록을 정리했다.

compile.en: (computing) translate program code into a form a computer can run

ex.en: The program failed to compile due to a syntax error.
ex.ko: 그 프로그램은 문법 오류 때문에 컴파일에 실패했다.

completion.en: the act of finishing something completely

ex.en: The project is nearing completion.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 완료에 가까워지고 있다.

complexion.en: the natural color and appearance of the skin on someone's face

ex.en: She has a clear, healthy complexion.
ex.ko: 그녀는 맑고 건강한 안색을 가지고 있다.

complexion.en: the general nature or character of a situation

ex.en: The new evidence changed the complexion of the case.
ex.ko: 새로운 증거가 그 사건의 양상을 바꿔놓았다.

complexity.en: the state of having many interconnected parts, making something difficult to understand

ex.en: The complexity of the tax system confuses many people.
ex.ko: 세금 제도의 복잡성이 많은 사람들을 혼란스럽게 한다.

component.en: one of the parts that together make up a whole system or object

ex.en: Trust is a key component of a good relationship.
ex.ko: 신뢰는 좋은 관계의 핵심 구성요소이다.

comprehension.en: the ability to understand something, especially spoken or written language

ex.en: The exam includes a reading comprehension section.
ex.ko: 그 시험에는 독해력(읽기 이해력) 섹션이 포함되어 있다.

comprehensive.en: including everything that is necessary or relevant; complete

ex.en: The report gives a comprehensive overview of the market.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 시장에 대한 포괄적인 개요를 제공한다.

compress.en: a pad of cloth pressed against part of the body to reduce pain or swelling

ex.en: She put a cold compress on the bruise.
ex.ko: 그녀는 멍든 곳에 찬 찜질팩을 댔다.

compress.en: press something firmly together to make it smaller or more compact

ex.en: You can compress the file to save space.
ex.ko: 파일을 압축하면 용량을 아낄 수 있다.

compression.en: the process of making something smaller or more compact, including reducing digital file size

ex.en: This software offers high-quality image compression.
ex.ko: 이 소프트웨어는 고화질 이미지 압축 기능을 제공한다.

compulsory.en: required by law or rule; must be done

ex.en: Wearing a seatbelt is compulsory in this country.
ex.ko: 이 나라에서는 안전벨트 착용이 의무이다.

compute.en: work out a number or amount using mathematics

ex.en: The software computes the total cost automatically.
ex.ko: 그 소프트웨어는 자동으로 총 비용을 계산한다.

conceal.en: prevent something from being seen or known

ex.en: She tried to conceal her disappointment.
ex.ko: 그녀는 실망감을 숨기려 애썼다.

concede.en: admit, often reluctantly, that something is true

ex.en: She conceded that his plan made sense.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그의 계획이 타당하다는 것을 마지못해 인정했다.

concede.en: admit that you have lost a contest or election

ex.en: The candidate conceded the election after the votes were counted.
ex.ko: 그 후보자는 개표 후 패배를 인정했다.

conceit.en: excessive pride in yourself or your abilities

ex.en: His conceit made him unpopular with his coworkers.
ex.ko: 그의 자만심 때문에 그는 동료들 사이에서 인기가 없었다.

conceivable.en: able to be imagined or believed

ex.en: It's conceivable that the plan could fail.
ex.ko: 그 계획이 실패할 가능성도 있을 법하다.

conceive.en: form an idea or plan in your mind

ex.en: She conceived the idea for the novel while traveling.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행 중에 그 소설에 대한 아이디어를 떠올렸다.

ex.en: They had been trying to conceive for a year.
ex.ko: 그들은 1년 동안 임신하려고 노력해왔다.

conception.en: an idea or plan formed in the mind

ex.en: His conception of a perfect vacation is very different from mine.
ex.ko: 완벽한 휴가에 대한 그의 구상은 나와 매우 다르다.

concerning.en: about a particular subject or thing

ex.en: I have a question concerning your last email.
ex.ko: 당신의 지난 이메일에 관해 질문이 있어요.

concession.en: something you agree to allow or give up in order to end a disagreement

ex.en: Both sides made concessions to reach an agreement.
ex.ko: 양측 모두 합의에 이르기 위해 양보했다.

concrete.en: a hard building material made from sand, gravel, cement, and water

ex.en: They poured concrete for the new driveway.
ex.ko: 그들은 새 진입로에 콘크리트를 부었다.

concrete.cue: 추상적이지 않고 실제로 보고 만질 수 있는
concrete.en: specific and real, not abstract or vague

ex.en: We need concrete evidence, not just guesses.
ex.ko: 우리는 추측이 아니라 구체적인 증거가 필요하다.

condemn.en: express strong disapproval of something, especially publicly

ex.en: World leaders condemned the attack.
ex.ko: 세계 각국 지도자들은 그 공격을 규탄했다.

condemn.en: officially declare a building unfit for use

ex.en: The old bridge was condemned after the inspection.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 다리는 점검 후 사용 불가 판정을 받았다.

conductor.en: a person who directs a group of musicians, especially an orchestra

ex.en: The conductor raised his baton to begin the symphony.
ex.ko: 지휘자는 교향곡을 시작하기 위해 지휘봉을 들었다.

conductor.en: a person whose job is to check tickets on a train or bus

ex.en: The conductor checked everyone's ticket.
ex.ko: 차장은 모두의 표를 확인했다.

conductor.en: a material that allows electricity or heat to pass through it easily

ex.en: Copper is an excellent conductor of electricity.
ex.ko: 구리는 뛰어난 전기 도체이다.

cone.en: a shape with a circular base that narrows to a point

ex.en: The road was blocked off with orange cones.
ex.ko: 그 도로는 주황색 원뿔형 표지로 막혀 있었다.

cone.en: a crisp, cone-shaped wafer used to hold ice cream

ex.en: She ordered a chocolate ice cream cone.
ex.ko: 그녀는 초콜릿 아이스크림 콘을 주문했다.

conference.en: a large formal meeting where people discuss ideas or share information on a topic

ex.en: She presented her research at an international conference.
ex.ko: 그녀는 국제 학회에서 자신의 연구를 발표했다.

conferencing.en: the practice of holding a meeting between people in different places using video or audio technology

ex.en: The team relies on video conferencing for remote meetings.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 원격 회의를 위해 화상회의에 의존한다.

confess.en: admit that you have done something wrong or reveal a secret

ex.en: He confessed to stealing the money.
ex.ko: 그는 돈을 훔쳤다고 자백했다.

confession.en: a statement admitting that you have done something wrong

ex.en: The suspect signed a written confession.
ex.ko: 용의자는 서면 자백서에 서명했다.

confidently.en: in a way that shows belief in your own abilities

ex.en: She answered the question confidently.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신 있게 질문에 답했다.

confine.en: limit something to a particular range, extent, or amount

ex.en: Please confine your comments to the topic at hand.
ex.ko: 발언은 지금 다루는 주제로 제한해 주세요.

confine.en: keep someone shut inside a place and prevent them from leaving

ex.en: The prisoner was confined to a small cell.
ex.ko: 그 죄수는 작은 감방에 갇혔다.

confront.en: deal with a difficult situation or problem directly

ex.en: We must confront this issue before it gets worse.
ex.ko: 이 문제가 더 심각해지기 전에 우리는 맞서야 한다.

confront.en: approach someone directly and challenge them about something, often angrily

ex.en: She confronted her coworker about the missing files.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사라진 파일에 대해 동료에게 따졌다.

conjunction.en: (grammar) a word that connects words, phrases, or clauses, such as 'and' or 'but'

ex.en: 'And' and 'but' are common conjunctions.
ex.ko: 'and'와 'but'은 흔히 쓰이는 접속사이다.

conjunction.en: the combination of two or more things acting together

ex.en: The medicine works best in conjunction with rest.
ex.ko: 그 약은 휴식과 결합될 때 가장 효과가 좋다.

conquest.en: the act of taking control of a country or territory by force

ex.en: The Roman conquest of Britain lasted for centuries.
ex.ko: 로마의 브리튼 정복은 수 세기 동안 이어졌다.

conscience.en: the inner sense that tells you whether your actions are right or wrong

ex.en: His conscience wouldn't let him lie.
ex.ko: 그의 양심이 거짓말을 하도록 놔두지 않았다.

consciousness.en: the state of being awake and aware of your surroundings

ex.en: He lost consciousness after the fall.
ex.ko: 그는 넘어진 후 의식을 잃었다.

consensus.en: general agreement among a group of people

ex.en: The team reached a consensus on the new plan.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 새 계획에 대해 합의에 이르렀다.

ex.en: The doctor needs your consent before the surgery.
ex.ko: 의사는 수술 전에 당신의 동의가 필요하다.

considerably.en: by a large amount; to a great degree

ex.en: Prices have risen considerably this year.
ex.ko: 올해 물가가 상당히 올랐다.

considering.en: taking a particular fact into account

ex.en: Considering the weather, the trip went well.
ex.ko: 날씨를 감안하면 여행은 잘 진행되었다.

consistent.en: always behaving or happening in the same way

ex.en: She has been consistent in her performance all season.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시즌 내내 일관된 활약을 보였다.

consolation.en: a feeling of comfort when you are sad or disappointed

ex.en: It was a small consolation that at least no one was hurt.
ex.ko: 적어도 다친 사람이 없다는 게 작은 위안이었다.

console.en: give comfort to someone who is sad or disappointed

ex.en: She tried to console her friend after the breakup.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이별 후 친구를 위로하려 애썼다.

console.en: a device with controls for playing electronic games or operating a machine

ex.en: He bought the latest gaming console.
ex.ko: 그는 최신 게임 콘솔을 샀다.

ex.en: She lives with constant pain in her back.
ex.ko: 그녀는 끊임없는 허리 통증을 안고 산다.

constant.en: a value or quantity that does not change

ex.en: Pi is a mathematical constant.
ex.ko: 파이(π)는 수학 상수이다.

consult.en: go to someone, especially an expert, for advice or information

ex.en: You should consult a doctor about that pain.
ex.ko: 그 통증에 대해 의사와 상담해야 한다.

consult.en: look at a book, map, or other source of information

ex.en: I consulted the manual before starting the machine.
ex.ko: 나는 기계를 작동하기 전에 설명서를 참고했다.

consultant.en: a person who is paid to give expert advice to an organization or business

ex.en: They hired a consultant to improve their marketing strategy.
ex.ko: 그들은 마케팅 전략을 개선하기 위해 컨설턴트를 고용했다.

contaminate.en: make something dirty or impure by adding a harmful substance

ex.en: The chemical spill contaminated the river.
ex.ko: 그 화학물질 유출이 강을 오염시켰다.

contemporary.en: belonging to or happening in the present time

ex.en: The museum focuses on contemporary art.
ex.ko: 그 미술관은 현대 미술에 중점을 둔다.

contemporary.en: living or happening during the same period of time as someone or something else

ex.en: Shakespeare was contemporary with Cervantes.
ex.ko: 셰익스피어는 세르반테스와 동시대 사람이었다.

contempt.en: a strong feeling that someone or something deserves no respect

ex.en: She looked at him with contempt.
ex.ko: 그녀는 경멸의 눈빛으로 그를 바라보았다.

contract.en: a formal written agreement between two or more parties that is legally binding

ex.en: They signed a contract to rent the apartment.
ex.ko: 그들은 아파트를 임대하는 계약서에 서명했다.

ex.en: He contracted a serious illness while traveling abroad.
ex.ko: 그는 해외 여행 중 심각한 병에 걸렸다.

ex.en: Metal contracts as it cools.
ex.ko: 금속은 식으면서 수축한다.

contradict.en: say the opposite of what someone else has said, or be inconsistent with it

ex.en: His actions contradict his words.
ex.ko: 그의 행동은 그의 말과 모순된다.

contradiction.en: a situation in which two facts, ideas, or statements are opposed to each other

ex.en: There's a contradiction between what he said and what he did.
ex.ko: 그가 말한 것과 행동한 것 사이에 모순이 있다.

contradictory.cue: 두 가지가 동시에 참일 수 없는
contradictory.en: containing ideas or statements that disagree with each other

ex.en: The witnesses gave contradictory accounts of the accident.
ex.ko: 목격자들은 사고에 대해 서로 모순되는 진술을 했다.

controversy.en: public disagreement or argument about something

ex.en: The decision caused a lot of controversy.
ex.ko: 그 결정은 많은 논란을 일으켰다.

conveniently.en: in a way that is easy or suitable for a particular purpose

ex.en: The hotel is conveniently located near the station.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 역 근처에 편리하게 위치해 있다.

convention.en: a traditional way of behaving that is widely accepted in a society

ex.en: It's a social convention to shake hands when meeting someone.
ex.ko: 누군가를 만날 때 악수하는 것은 사회적 관습이다.

convention.en: a large formal meeting of people who share the same interest or profession

ex.en: Thousands attended the annual comic convention.
ex.ko: 수천 명이 연례 코믹 컨벤션에 참석했다.

conventional.en: following what is traditional or generally accepted, rather than new or original

ex.en: She prefers conventional methods over new technology.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새로운 기술보다 전통적인 방식을 선호한다.

conventional.en: (of weapons) using regular explosives rather than nuclear power

ex.en: The two countries agreed to reduce conventional weapons.
ex.ko: 두 나라는 재래식 무기를 줄이기로 합의했다.

converse.en: the exact opposite of a statement or situation

ex.en: The converse of this rule is also true.
ex.ko: 이 규칙의 정반대도 참이다.

ex.en: They conversed for hours about politics.
ex.ko: 그들은 몇 시간 동안 정치에 대해 대화를 나눴다.

conversely.en: used to introduce a statement that is the opposite of what was just said

ex.en: Some people love spicy food; conversely, others can't handle it at all.
ex.ko: 어떤 사람들은 매운 음식을 좋아하지만, 반대로 어떤 사람들은 전혀 못 먹는다.

conversion.en: the process of changing something from one form, use, or unit to another

ex.en: The conversion from Celsius to Fahrenheit requires a formula.
ex.ko: 섭씨에서 화씨로의 변환에는 공식이 필요하다.

convert.en: change something from one form, use, or system to another

ex.en: They converted the old barn into a house.
ex.ko: 그들은 오래된 헛간을 집으로 개조했다.

convert.en: change your religion or beliefs, or persuade someone else to

ex.en: He converted to Buddhism in his thirties.
ex.ko: 그는 30대에 불교로 개종했다.

convinced.en: completely sure that something is true

ex.en: I'm convinced that she's telling the truth.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀가 진실을 말하고 있다고 확신한다.

cooperate.en: work together with someone to achieve a shared goal

ex.en: The two departments cooperated to finish the project on time.
ex.ko: 두 부서는 프로젝트를 제때 끝내기 위해 협력했다.

cooperation.en: the act of working together with others toward a shared goal

ex.en: The project succeeded thanks to close cooperation between teams.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트는 팀 간의 긴밀한 협력 덕분에 성공했다.

cooperative.en: willing to help and work together with others

ex.en: The staff were very cooperative during the audit.
ex.ko: 직원들은 감사 기간 동안 매우 협조적이었다.

cooperative.en: a business or organization owned and run jointly by its members

ex.en: The farmers formed a cooperative to sell their produce.
ex.ko: 농부들은 농산물을 팔기 위해 협동조합을 만들었다.

cope.en: deal successfully with a difficult situation

ex.en: She struggled to cope with the stress of the new job.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 직장의 스트레스에 대처하느라 힘들어했다.

copper.en: a reddish-brown metal that conducts electricity well

ex.en: Copper wires are used in electrical systems.
ex.ko: 구리 전선은 전기 시스템에 사용된다.

coral.en: a hard structure formed in the sea by the skeletons of small marine animals

ex.en: They went diving to see the coral reef.
ex.ko: 그들은 산호초를 보러 다이빙을 하러 갔다.

ex.en: The earth's core is extremely hot.
ex.ko: 지구의 중심부(핵)는 매우 뜨겁다.

core.en: the most important or essential part of an idea or issue

ex.en: Honesty is at the core of any good relationship.
ex.ko: 정직함은 어떤 좋은 관계에서든 핵심이다.

cornerstone.cue: 어떤 것의 가장 기본이 되는 바탕
cornerstone.en: the basic principle or fact on which something important is built

ex.en: Trust is the cornerstone of a strong team.
ex.ko: 신뢰는 강한 팀의 초석이다.

cornet.en: a brass musical instrument similar to a trumpet, with a mellower sound

ex.en: He learned to play the cornet in the school band.
ex.ko: 그는 학교 밴드에서 코넷 연주를 배웠다.

corona.en: the outer layer of gas surrounding the sun, visible during a solar eclipse

ex.en: You can see the sun's corona during a total eclipse.
ex.ko: 개기일식 때 태양의 코로나를 볼 수 있다.

corporal.en: relating to the human body, not the mind

ex.en: Corporal punishment is banned in most schools now.
ex.ko: 대부분의 학교에서 이제 체벌(신체적 처벌)이 금지되어 있다.

corporal.en: a low-ranking officer in the army or marines

ex.en: The corporal led the small patrol unit.
ex.ko: 그 하사는 작은 순찰대를 이끌었다.

corporate.en: relating to a large company or business

ex.en: She works in the corporate finance department.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기업 재무 부서에서 일한다.

corporation.en: a large company or group of companies recognized by law as a single unit

ex.en: He works for a multinational corporation.
ex.ko: 그는 다국적 기업에서 일한다.

correctness.en: the quality of being accurate and free from errors

ex.en: Please check the correctness of these figures.
ex.ko: 이 수치들의 정확성을 확인해 주세요.

correspond.en: match or be similar to something else

ex.en: His story doesn't correspond with what she said.
ex.ko: 그의 이야기는 그녀가 말한 것과 일치하지 않는다.

correspond.en: communicate with someone regularly by exchanging letters or emails

ex.en: They corresponded for years after moving apart.
ex.ko: 그들은 떨어져 산 이후에도 몇 년간 서신을 주고받았다.

correspondence.en: the exchange of letters or messages with someone

ex.en: They kept up a correspondence for many years.
ex.ko: 그들은 여러 해 동안 서신을 주고받았다.

correspondent.en: a journalist who reports news from a particular place, especially abroad

ex.en: The correspondent reported live from the disaster zone.
ex.ko: 그 특파원은 재난 현장에서 생중계로 보도했다.

corresponding.en: matching or similar in position or purpose to something else

ex.en: Each number has a corresponding letter.
ex.ko: 각 숫자에는 상응하는 문자가 있다.

corridor.cue: 방들 사이를 잇는 좁고 긴 통로
corridor.en: a long, narrow passage in a building connecting rooms

ex.en: He waited in the corridor outside the office.
ex.ko: 그는 사무실 밖 복도에서 기다렸다.

corrupt.en: willing to act dishonestly, especially in exchange for money or power

ex.en: The corrupt official was arrested for taking bribes.
ex.ko: 그 부패한 공무원은 뇌물을 받은 혐의로 체포되었다.

corrupt.en: (of a computer file) damaged so that it cannot be used properly

ex.en: The file was corrupt and wouldn't open.
ex.ko: 그 파일은 손상되어 열리지 않았다.
